substitutionRules: dev semantic-dev uses explicit in-memory providerClass for eligible runtime capabilities and must satisfy the same semantic port contract as the real provider
test uses compose-local or real local providers and must prove parity with the in-memory provider contract
staging remains prod-shaped sandbox; no in-memory provider may be used unless classified non-runtime/static
prod remains live/readiness-only; no in-memory provider may be used for runtime capability execution
provider substitution must be documented in this matrix and the generated in-memory provider coverage reports
